raw_description: Communications Director Status: Full-time, Exempt Salary Range (annual): $85,000 - $110,000 Schedule: 40 hours per week, Monday-Friday, 8 a.m.- 5 p.m. Pacific Time Veloz is based in Sacramento, California. This is a 100% remote work position in the U.S. with a prioritization given to candidates based on the West Coast. Travel within and/or to California and nationwide is expected up to 5% annually. About Veloz Veloz is a national nonpartisan (c)(3) nonprofit with a vision of the future where transportation is electric for all. We drive toward that all-electric future through strategic communications, unprecedented collaborations and purposeful convening. Veloz is the power behind the nation’s largest and most inventive multi-partner education campaign for electric vehicles, the architect of events and programming garnering national attention, and the organization bringing together a high-powered, diverse Board of Directors, Public Policy Board and members from the public and private sectors. Opportunity and Position Description In this position, the Content Marketing Manager is part of a passionate and collaborative organization that is changing the conversation about EVs nationwide by sparking a virtuous cycle of consumer awareness and demand. Veloz plays a unique and important role in the national EV landscape, and now is the time to take our efforts to an exponentially more impactful level. This requires a clear and inspiring vision, sophisticated strategies, significant and sustained financial support, and smart implementation. Reporting to the Communications Director and partnering with the Veloz team, the Content Marketing Manager leads Veloz’s industry-facing and consumer facing social, newsletter and digital content across several platforms. This focus may require the ability to develop design assets. This individual plays a critical role in making the electric for all vision a reality by executing aspects of a comprehensive communications and marketing strategy to raise awareness of Veloz and Electric For All, developing and delivering high-quality and high-value content and working alongside the Communications team to manage aspects of our industry-focused and consumer-facing communications efforts. In addition, this individual will provide a supporting role to communications leadership on -day communications activities Job Duties Social Media Marketing - Develop and maintain a social media calendar with strategic content, posts and analytics. - Post content, manage and maintain all social media profiles for Veloz and Electric For All, growing our online presence and influence. - When applicable, Work with Veloz’s agency contractors to develop organic social support. - Manage and produce user generated content, and community management for both Veloz and Electric For All social channels - Actively participate in Veloz and Electric For All events by attending and developing social media activities and post-event messaging. Content Creation & Marketing - Create and deploy strong written and visual communications to generate timely content and updates for newsletters, both websites, social media, reports, and other materials distributed across Veloz and Electric For All marketing channels — including layout/design elements and A/B testing. - Provide event support via communications channels including, but not limited to digital asset creation, updating events on Veloz.org, and creating social media posts and graphics. - Research and implement content marketing and social media strategies to increase our reach and impact, while being responsive and timely to trends, current, member messaging and developments with social media posts. - Develop and produce an inventory of visual assets, including photos, videos and branding. - Ensure that the Veloz and Electric For All brands/tone are consistent across all media. Audience Growth & Engagement - Work with the communications team on a data-informed strategy to grow the Veloz and Electric For All social media channels and newsletters, while increasing website traffic and conversions on Veloz.org and ElectricForAll.org. - Manage, maintain a record of engagement and growth across all social media platforms for Veloz and Electric For All. - Manage and update Veloz and Electric for All email database including press, members, business development leads, event attendees, etc. - Analyze and present metrics for Veloz and Electric For All newsletters, social media channels and website to the team to help ensure a continuous virtuous cycle between our programs and our marketing efforts. - Engage with contact form outreach from both ElectricForAll.org and Veloz.org by responding to questions, recommending Veloz’s tools and services, and pointing people toward resources. Additional Support - Provide -day support to the communications leads. - Be available to support presentations and other materials supporting member engagement as needed. - Manage social media and design-related vendor contracts and process invoices as needed. - Be available to participate in key industry conferences, workshops and seminars, creating assets and activations on behalf of Veloz when appropriate. - Promote a culture of collaboration, communication, creativity with high performance standards along with, continual learning and a commitment to excellence. - Support the team in creating and ordering Veloz swag as needed for in-person events and other Veloz needs. Reports to: Communications Director Status: Full-time, Exempt Salary Range (annual): $85,000 - $110,000 Schedule: 40 hours per week, Monday-Friday, 8 a.m.- 5 p.m. Pacific Time Location: Veloz is based in Sacramento, California. This is a 100% remote work position in the U.S. with a prioritization given to candidates based on the West Coast. Travel within and/or to California and nationwide is expected up to 5% annually. title: Audience Acquisition Executive
pay_candidate_snippets: Creating complex, high impact hybrid and digital events, and engaging our 140,000 members globally, we partner with an incredible ecosystem of 1,000 impact partners, UN Agencies, and private sector companies.
raw_description: Climate Action is a mission-led convening platform accelerating global climate solutions by connecting leaders across business, finance, policy, and civil society. This is a unique opportunity to work at the heart of that network engaging senior stakeholders shaping key global moments such as the UNFCCC Conference of the Parties (COP) and London Climate Action Week. You’ll help bring together decision-makers from governments, UN agencies, investors, business and industry to drive real-world positive impact. As part of the delegate acquisition team, you’ll support the full audience journey. From outreach and invitations through to registration and attendance. Helping deliver a seamless, high-quality experience for our global community. You’ll play a key role in growing and engaging our network of 120K+ sustainability professionals by identifying new audiences, managing communications, and building strong relationships across our events portfolio. This role is ideally suited to a highly organised and proactive individual with excellent interpersonal skills and the confidence to engage effectively with senior stakeholders. The purpose of this role is to connect and engage high-level individuals who hold the influence and capacity to drive positive change. The successful candidate will demonstrate a strong aptitude for relationship-building and a genuine interest in mission-led work. This position offers valuable hands-on experience in the delivery of high-profile, purpose-driven events within a dynamic and fast-paced environment. It presents an excellent opportunity for a candidate with an interest in sustainability and climate action to develop professionally, broaden their network, and contribute to meaningful and lasting impact. Key Responsibilities - Execute targeted audience acquisition campaigns across email,, CRM platforms, and partner channels to drive qualified delegate registration - Support the planning and optimisation of multi-channel delegate acquisition campaigns, using data and insights to improve performance and delegate (including VIP guesting) conversion rates - Assist in managing the registration journey, including sending out invitations, RSVP tracking, confirmations, and pre-event engagement communications - Monitor campaign and registration performance against KPIs, identifying trends and making data-driven recommendations to improve results - Segment and maintain delegate data within CRM systems to enable effective targeting, personalisation, and reporting - Create and schedule compelling audience communications, including invitations, reminders, and pre-event content - Track, analyse and report engagement metrics (open rates, click-through rates, conversion) to continuously refine outreach strategies - Collaborate with marketing, production, and commercial teams to align audience targeting with event positioning and value propositions - Manage inbound enquiries and delegate communications, ensuring a professional and responsive experience - Support onboarding of delegates onto event platforms to increase engagement and networking participation - Contribute to post-campaign and post-event analysis, including performance reporting, attendance tracking, and audience insights Established in 2007 and headquartered in London, Climate Action has been working at the intersection of climate change policy, innovation, and finance. title: TikTok Specialist
pay_candidate_snippets: We achieve these goals through open source research and social media analysis, digital investigations, building the capacity of local partners, and collaborating with media to amplify the impact of our work.

The Centre for Information Resilience (CIR) is an independent, non-profit social enterprise dedicated to exposing human rights abuses and war crimes, countering disinformation, and combating online behaviour harmful to women and minorities.
The consultant will provide expert guidance on TikTok platform developments, algorithm changes, content optimisation, and performance analysis to support the project's objectives.

About the Role We’re looking for a creative and data-driven TikTok Specialist to provide ongoing strategic support to strengthen the performance, reach, and engagement of the project's TikTok presence.

The Centre for Information Resilience (CIR) is an independent organisation dedicated to exposing human rights violations and threats to democracy. We achieve this through open source research, digital investigations, capacity-building with local partners, and collaboration with m
raw_description: The Centre for Information Resilience (CIR) is an independent organisation dedicated to exposing human rights violations and threats to democracy. We achieve this through open source research, digital investigations, capacity-building with local partners, and collaboration with media to amplify our impact. CIR was born out of a determination to expose those spreading harm – online and offline – around the world, particularly in areas of violent conflict, such as Ukraine, Sudan and Myanmar. Our projects are at the forefront of efforts to investigate and document human rights abuses, war crimes, harms targeting women and minorities, and influence operations. We work closely with multilateral and national justice accountability bodies. Contract Type: Contractor Contract length: 1 year - 4-5 days per month. Hours needed Part-time (4–5 days per month; daily working hours are negotiable and can be arranged to suit the successful candidate). Remote. Languages: You must be able to speak and write English and French. Closing date: 1 July 2026 at 23:55pm. Hours needed Part-time (4–5 days per month; daily working hours are negotiable and can be arranged to suit the successful candidate). . Languages: You must be able to speak and write English and French. Closing date: 1 July 2026 at 23:55pm. About the Role We’re looking for a creative and data-driven TikTok Specialist to provide ongoing strategic support to strengthen the performance, reach, and engagement of the project's TikTok presence. The consultant will provide expert guidance on TikTok platform developments, algorithm changes, content optimisation, and performance analysis to support the project's objectives. The Centre for Information Resilience (CIR) is an independent, non-profit social enterprise dedicated to exposing human rights abuses and war crimes, countering disinformation, and combating online behaviour harmful to women and minorities. We achieve these goals through open source research and social media analysis, digital investigations, building the capacity of local partners, and collaborating with media to amplify the impact of our work

title: Director, Digital Advocacy and Engagement
location: New York, New York, United States
pay_candidate_snippets: Today, the Innocence Project continues to fight for freedom, drive structural change, and advance the innocence movement.
Our efforts have led to the passage of more than 200 transformative state laws and federal reforms.
raw_description: ABOUT THE INNOCENCE PROJECT The Innocence Project works to free the innocent, prevent wrongful convictions, and create fair, compassionate, and equitable systems of justice for everyone. Our work is guided by science and grounded in anti-racism. Since our inception, the Innocence Project has used DNA and other scientific advancements to prove wrongful conviction. To date, we have helped to free or exonerate more than people who, collectively, spent more than 3, years behind bars. Our efforts have led to the passage of more than 200 transformative state laws and federal reforms. Today, the Innocence Project continues to fight for freedom, drive structural change, and advance the innocence movement. To, please visit www.innocenceproject.org. ABOUT THE POSITION The Innocence Project recognizes the immense power of strategic communications to advance legal and policy initiatives to free the innocent, prevent wrongful convictions and achieve criminal legal system reform. Reporting to the Chief Communications Officer, the Director, Digital Advocacy and Engagement leads the Innocence Project’s Webby-, Shorty-, and Telly Award-winning digital communications team and is responsible for growing and mobilizing our more than 5 million supporter community. The role drives pivotal campaigns, multiplatform storytelling, and grassroots mobilization to move supporters from awareness to action and build pressure for systemic change. The Innocence Projects’s digital platforms are key to amplifying the organization’s most consequential public moments — including exonerations, execution and clemency campaigns, policy wins and reforms, and rapid responses to breaking. They are key to growing and elevating the Innocence Project’s brand and profile as a leader in criminal justice reform. In collaboration with the Director of Digital Fundraising, the Director, Digital Advocacy and Engagement is responsible for the organization’s digital channels (inbound, outbound, paid, and earned). The Director, Digital Advocacy and Engagement reports to the Chief Communications Officer, directly supervises the Digital Engagement Specialist, Digital Coordinator, and consultant team, and partners closely with the Director of Digital Fundraising in the development department. They also have close working relationships with other members of the entire communications department, including the editorial, creative, marketing, and media teams, and with colleagues across our programmatic areas, including policy, legal, science & research, and social work. We are interested in every qualified candidate who is eligible to work in the United States. However, we are not able to sponsor visas and we are not an e-verify employer. The duties of this position require approximately 40 hours of work per week to be performed virtually/in-office. We currently have a hybrid in-office model, with all employees in the office three days per week and the remainder of the week remotely. Wednesday is our organization’s “anchor day” where all staff works in the office. The number of required in-office days is subject to change. THE IDEAL CANDIDATE The Innocence Project seeks a digital campaigns leader with a strong track record in digital advocacy campaigns and/or political communications and is skilled in digital strategy, breaking and rapid response. They are a big-picture strategic thinker who is also mindful of the details. They are a collaborative, values-driven professional who can translate complex issues into compelling content and messaging - both editorially and visually - and who is committed to the Innocence Project’s mission. They have a natural curiosity about what’s new in digital advocacy, an appetite for experimentation, and a love of growing and mentoring their team members. In addition, the ideal candidate models our IP Values of accountability, collaboration, empathy + compassion, equity, person-centeredness, quality focus, and self-awareness. All Innocence Project employees must embrace, respect and value the rich diversity of perspectives and lived experience of our staff. We recognize and appreciate that our work is better for having varied participants and thought partners. The ideal candidate is someone who thrives in this type of environment. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS - Lead digital strategy and content for the Innocence Project's highest-stakes communications campaigns including execution and clemency cases, state-based policy reforms, and rapid response to breaking developments. - Ability to collaborate and partner with colleagues in the legal, social work, and policy teams as well as external partner organizations along with colleagues in the communications team. Commitment to centering the experiences of clients and those in the freed and exonerated community who are closest to the issues we are addressing. - Translate complex legal and policy issues into accurate, compelling, and accessible public calls to action across the organization’s digital channels (in English and Spanish, where campaigns demand). - Build grassroots power to convert supporters into advocates and mobilize them to act when it matters most through petitions, calls, emails to decision-makers and during rapid-response moments. - Maintain and strengthen a rapid-response infrastructure (templates, escalation paths, and on-call team coverage) that allows the organization to mount campaigns with tight deadlines. - Manage and develop the digital team including the Digital Engagement Specialist and Digital Coordinator, with clear goals, work plans, and a culture that prioritizes both excellent work and well-being. In partnership with the Director of Digital Fundraising, manage and oversee a team of consultants who are deeply engaged in the communications and digital fundraising teams’ -day operations. - Track and analyze digital communications data to surface what is working and where opportunities lie for setting the IP apart and breaking through the noise on digital platforms. - Stay abreast of digital trends to ensure our strategies and content are primed for maximum impact. This includes attention to the look and feel of our content along with increasing our searchability and impact. It would also encompass strengthening discoverability across both search (SEO) and AI-generated responses (GEO) The Innocence Project works to free the innocent, prevent wrongful convictions, and create fair, compassionate, and equitable systems of justice for everyone. Our work is guided by science and grounded in anti-racism. Since our inception, the Innocence Project has used DNA and other scientific advancements to prove wrongful conviction. To date, we have helped to free or exonerate more than 200 people who, collectively, spent more than 3, years behind bars. Our efforts have led to the passage of more than 200 transformative state laws and federal reforms. Today, the Innocence Project continues to fight for freedom, drive structural change, and advance the innocence movement.
